meta.title: Thai Reading Guide - Practical Examples
meta.description: Practical guide for reading Thai text with step-by-step examples

tips: Thai has aspirated and unaspirated consonants - aspirated sounds have a puff of air
Some consonants sound different at the beginning vs end of syllables
Practice moving your tongue to different positions in your mouth
common_mistakes: Don't confuse ก (g/k) with ค (kh) - the second has more breath
Thai 'r' is rolled, but often pronounced as 'l' in casual speech
Final consonants are not released - stop the sound abruptly

tips: Thai distinguishes short and long vowels - length affects meaning
Some vowels are written around the consonant, not just after it
The 'ue' sound (◌ึ, ◌ือ) is like Russian 'ы' - central unrounded vowel

tone_pronunciation.description: How to produce the five Thai tones

techniques.mid_tone: Flat, neutral pitch - like stating a fact
techniques.low_tone: Low, level pitch - like being disappointed
techniques.falling_tone: Start high, drop to low - like saying 'oh!' in surprise
techniques.rising_tone: Start low, rise high - like asking a question in English

step_by_step.title: Identify consonants
step_by_step.description: Find all consonant letters and determine their class

example.analysis: ข (high class) + า + ว (low class final)

step_by_step.title: Identify vowels
step_by_step.description: Find vowel marks and determine the vowel sound

step_by_step.title: Determine syllable type
step_by_step.description: Check if syllable is live (open/sonorant ending) or dead (closed)

step_by_step.title: Apply tone rules
step_by_step.description: Use consonant class + syllable type + tone marks to find tone

example.analysis: High class + live syllable + no tone mark = rising tone

step_by_step.title: Combine sounds
step_by_step.description: Put together: initial consonant + vowel + final + tone

consonant_clusters.pronunciation: ร often silent or very light
ล often silent in casual speech

reading_passages.title: Simple Greetings
reading_passages.thai: สวัสดี ครับ ผม ชื่อ จอห์น คุณ ชื่อ อะไร ครับ
reading_passages.transcription: sàwàtdii khráp phǒm chɯ̂ɯ joohn khun chɯ̂ɯ àrai khráp
reading_passages.cyrillic: сàвàтдии кхрáп пхǒм чɯ̂ɯ джоон кхун чɯ̂ɯ àрай кхрáп
reading_passages.meaning: Hello sir/madam. My name is John. What is your name, sir?
breakdown: ชื่อ - name/to be named

common_difficulties.solution: Practice writing and pay attention to small differences
examples: ต vs ด - ต has a loop, ด doesn't
บ vs ป - บ is more rounded, ป has a straight line
ค vs ฅ - ฅ is obsolete, use ค

common_difficulties.solution: Memorize consonant classes first, then apply tone rules systematically
tips: High class consonants: ข ฉ ถ ผ ฝ ส ห (+ some rare ones)
Mid class consonants: ก จ ด ต บ ป อ

common_difficulties.solution: Practice with minimal pairs and native audio

learning_tips: Learn consonant classes before detailed tone rules
Practice writing by hand to reinforce memory
Focus on pronunciation from the beginning
Find patterns in spelling rather than memorizing every word
